input_row.title: Ensure cross-origin resource sharing does not allow all origins
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Service or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under API, click CORS. 4. Under Allowed Origins, delete the entry that equals *. 5. Specify the origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls. 6. Click Save. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command: az functionapp cors remove --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --allowed-origins "*" Use the following command to specify the origins that should be allowed: az functionapp cors add --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --allowed-origins <allowed-origins> Default Value: By default, Allowed Origins is set to https://portal.azure.com. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/app-service/app-service-web-tutorial- rest-api 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp/cors 3. https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Headers_Cheat_Sheet.ht ml 4. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Guides/CORS 5. https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross- Site_Request_Forgery_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The remediation section provides specific Azure CLI commands that can be utilized to verify and enforce the compliance control. This suggests an API-accessible property.
- The nature of the control pertains to CORS settings, which are standard configurations for Azure App Service and Function Apps and can be queried via the Azure APIs.
- Since the audit clearly mentions how to check CORS settings with the Azure CLI, this leads to the conclusion that the check can be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_functionapp_cors_allowed_origins_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check:
- Utilize Azure CLI commands to retrieve CORS settings for Function Apps.
- Enumerate all Function Apps in a specified resource group/subscription.
- Validate the `allowed-origins` property to ensure it does not include any entry that equals `*`.

**Pass/Fail criteria:**
- Pass if the CORS settings for each Function App have allowed origins populated with specific URLs rather than the wildcard `*`.
- Fail if any Function App has allowed origins set to `*`.

**Example CLI commands:**
1. To list current CORS settings for a Function App:
   ```
   az functionapp cors list --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>
   ```
   The output should be checked to confirm that it does not contain `*`.

2. To ensure compliance, if violations are found, run:
   ```
   az functionapp cors remove --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --allowed-origins "*"
   az functionapp cors add --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --allowed-origins <allowed-origins>
   ```

These commands will help enforce that only explicitly allowed origins are specified for cross-origin requests, ensuring compliance with the listed security controls around CORS.
